fundamentals.name: Roblox Corporation
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Roblox Corporation operates an immersive platform for connection and communication in the United States and internationally. It offers Roblox Client, an application that allows users to explore immersive experience; Roblox Studio, a free toolset that allows developers and creators to build, publish, and operate immersive experiences and other content; and Roblox Cloud, which provides services and infrastructure that power the platform. Roblox Corporation was incorporated in 2004 and is headquartered in San Mateo, California.

geographic.name: Asia Pacific Including Australia And New Zealand

judgment.summary: High-growth company with significant revenue expansion but persistent losses and high debt.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates; next earnings date is 2026-07-30.
positives: Revenue growth 39.3% YoY — strong top-line expansion.
Free cash flow (TTM) $1.20B — positive cash generation.
Analyst consensus is 'buy' with a mean target of $64.81.
negatives: Forward P/E of -37.40 indicates expected future losses.
Operating margin -16.4% and Profit margin -20.7% show significant operational losses.
Return on equity -311.9% and Return on assets -8.8% are deeply negative.
Debt/Equity ratio of 435.04 is very high.
Current ratio of 0.89 suggests potential short-term liquidity issues.

news.paragraph: Roblox shares are seeing some upward movement this week, following a period of weakness and analyst target adjustments. While one analyst from DA Davidson lowered their price target, other articles suggest the company may be undervalued and is still a better buy than GameStop. The company recently reported Q1 earnings that beat estimates, though forward-looking analyst estimates for the current quarter and fiscal year are modest.
